original_abstract: A three-dimensional network solid composed of Fe(III) centers and paramagnetic semiquinoid linkers, (NBu4)2Fe(III)2(dhbq)3 (dhbq(2-/3-) = 2,5-dioxidobenzoquinone/1,2-dioxido-4,5-semiquinone), is shown to exhibit a conductivity of 0.16 ± 0.01 S/cm at 298 K, representing one of the highest values reported to date for a metal-organic framework (MOF). Through a combination of spectroscopic techniques, slow-scan cyclic voltammetry, and variable-temperature magnetic and conductivity measurements, we identify the origin of this high electronic conductivity as ligand mixed-valency. Notably, UV-vis-NIR diffuse reflectance measurements provide the first observation of Robin-Day Class II/III mixed valency within a MOF. To explore the relationship between ligand redox states and material properties, we synthesized the reduced framework Na0.9(NBu4)1.8Fe(III)2(dhbq)3. A comparison between the two compounds reveals significant differences in electronic conductivity and magnetic ordering temperatures, which we correlate to the relative ratio of the ligand oxidation states. These findings establish transition metal-semiquinoid systems as a powerful platform for achieving and tuning long-range electronic communication in metal-organic frameworks.
humanized_abstract: The title compound, (NBu4)2Fe(III)2(dhbq)3 (dhbq(2-/3-) = 2,5-dioxidobenzoquinone/1,2-dioxido-4,5-semiquinone), is a three-dimensional network solid composed of paramagnetic iron(III) sites and electron-delocalized semiquinoid ligands. Electrical conductivity at ambient temperature is 0.16 ± 0.01 S/cm, one of the highest reported to date for a metal-organic framework. Analysis of magnetic and conductivity behavior in conjunction with detailed spectra determined from X-Band EPR (0.9 K to 300 K), UV-vis-NIR DF (25 K to 325 K), and slow-scan cyclic voltammetry reveal the networks to be electronically conductive based solely upon ligand mixed-valency. This is demonstrated to be a novel observation by the UV-vis-NIR DF measurements of the title compound, which become the first-in-class to display Robin-Day Class II/III mixed valency in a MOF. In an investigation to understand the effects of modifying ligand redox properties on MOF properties, Na0.9(NBu4)1.8Fe(III)2(dhbq)3 is synthesized in lesser solvent and shows significant different magnitudes of conductivity and distinct values of the magnetic ordering temperatures for both species.
